---
title: "Module 4: Query Optimization"
toc: true
toc-location: left
---

Practical techniques for writing faster, more efficient SQL queries.

## Use Indexes

- Add indexes on columns you frequently filter or join on
- Avoid full table scans on large datasets

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);
```

## Push Filters Early

```sql
-- Slow: joins first, then filters
SELECT * FROM orders
JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id
WHERE orders.status = 'completed';

-- Fast: filter before join
SELECT o.id, c.name
FROM (SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed') o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id;
```

## Read Query Plans

```sql
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'completed';
```

Look for: `Seq Scan` (slow) vs `Index Scan` (fast).
